text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Georgie.
zh: 我是乔治。

text: what do you think makes a good citizen?
zh: 你觉得什么样的人才算是好公民？

text: Ooh, interesting question.
zh: 哦，这个问题很有意思。

text: I think a good citizen is someone who doesn't break the law and is generally kind to other people.
zh: 我认为好公民就是不违法、并且总体上对他人友善的人。

text: What about you?
zh: 你呢？

text: Any ideas?
zh: 有什么想法吗？

text: Yeah, I agree.
zh: 是的，我同意。

text: But also maybe people who volunteer, so people who run clubs for children and that kind of thing.
zh: 但也许还包括那些做志愿者的人，比如为孩子们办俱乐部之类的人。

text: I think they're good citizens.
zh: 我觉得他们也是好公民。

text: Now, being a citizen, a member of society who enjoys certain rights in return for having certain responsibilities, is an old idea that's been in the news a lot recently.
zh: 公民身份，指的是社会成员在承担一定责任的同时享有某些权利，这是一个古老的概念，最近也频频出现在新闻中。

text: It's something that interests Myra Anubi, presenter of BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World.
zh: 这也是BBC世界频道节目《人们在解决世界问题》主持人玛拉·阿努比感兴趣的话题。

text: What do you think makes a good citizen?
zh: 你认为什么样的人才是好公民？

text: Is it someone who follows the laws of the land and pays their taxes?
zh: 是遵守国家法律、按时纳税的人吗？

text: Well, some people might say it should go beyond that.
zh: 有人可能会说，这还不够。

text: And a good citizen is one who tries to improve the place where they live.
zh: 好公民应该是努力改善自己所居住地方的人。

text: A good citizen obeys the law of the land, a term for the set of rules and laws that exists in a country.
zh: 好公民遵守“the law of the land”，也就是一个国家现行的一整套规则和法律。

text: But should there be more to it than that?
zh: 但公民责任是否应该不止于此？

text: Around the world, young people today are less likely to vote.
zh: 如今，世界各地的年轻人投票的可能性都在下降。

text: and many people aged 18 to 30 say they feel ignored by politicians.
zh: 许多18到30岁的人表示，他们感觉被政治家忽视了。

text: So in this programme we'll be hearing about MyPolis, an exciting project helping young people in Portugal re -engage with their citizenship.
zh: 因此，在这期节目中，我们将聊聊“MyPolis”，这是一个帮助葡萄牙年轻人重新参与公民生活的有趣项目。

text: And as usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases, all of which you'll find on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 像往常一样，我们也会学习一些实用的新单词和短语，都会发布在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上。

text: But before that, I have a question for you, Neil.
zh: 不过在那之前，我有个问题要问你，尼尔。

text: the project's name, Mypolis, comes from the ancient Greek states such as Athens and Sparta, where the idea of citizenship was born.
zh: 这个项目的名字“MyPolis”来源于古希腊城邦，比如雅典和斯巴达，那里正是公民概念的起源之地。

text: So, what is the meaning of the ancient Greek word polis?
zh: 那么，古希腊词“polis”的意思是什么呢？

text: Is it a vote, b people, or c city?
zh: A. 投票，B. 人民，还是C. 城市？

text: That's a good question.
zh: 这个问题不错。

text: I'm going to guess b people.
zh: 我猜是B，人民。

text: OK, well I'll reveal the correct answer later in the programme.
zh: 好的，我会在节目稍后公布正确答案。

text: MyPolis runs council assemblies where children can propose improvements to their local school community – anything from fixing broken water fountains to changing the curriculum.
zh: MyPolis组织议会会议，孩子们可以在会上提议改善当地学校社区的事项——从修理坏掉的饮水机到修改课程内容，无所不包。

text: Local politicians also attend, listening to the children's ideas and responding by giving their support or explaining why not.
zh: 当地政治家也会出席，聆听孩子们的想法，并做出回应，表示支持或解释为什么不支持。

text: The project was started by founder Bernardo Goncalves when he was in his 20s because he was disappointed by interactions between political parties and young people.
zh: 该项目由创始人贝尔纳多·贡萨尔维斯在他二十多岁时创立，因为他对政党与年轻人之间的互动感到失望。

text: Here's Bernardo telling more to BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World.
zh: 下面是贝尔纳多在BBC世界频道节目《人们在解决世界问题》中的讲述。

text: We have seen a quick erosion in the trust of the population on the democratic institutions such as the national parliament, such as the government in office.
zh: 我们看到公众对民主机构的信任正在迅速削弱，比如对国家议会、对在任政府的信任。

text: We need to find new ways to connect children and young citizens to democratic institutions.
zh: 我们需要找到新的方法，把儿童和年轻公民与民主机构联系起来。

text: Otherwise, the gap will get bigger and bigger.
zh: 否则，这个鸿沟只会越来越大。

text: this is very dangerous for democracy.
zh: 这对民主来说是非常危险的。

text: Bernardo thinks Portugal has seen an erosion of trust – a term for the gradual weakening of people's confidence in political and economic institutions – and the government in office.
zh: 贝尔纳多认为，葡萄牙经历了“信任的侵蚀”——指人们对政治和经济机构信心的逐渐减弱——以及对在任政府的信任危机。

text: A government in office means the government in power at the present time.
zh: “government in office”指的是当前掌权的政府。

text: MyPolice mirrors the political activities that children will need as adult citizens – things like voting and holding politicians accountable.
zh: MyPolis模拟了孩子们成年后作为公民所需的政治活动，比如投票和监督政治家履行职责。

text: Reporter Alison Roberts visited schools around the Portuguese capital Lisbon and was impressed by what she saw,
zh: 记者艾莉森·罗伯茨走访了葡萄牙首都里斯本周边的学校，她所看到的一切让她印象深刻，

text: as she explains here to Mayra Anubi, presenter of BBC World Service's People Fixing the World.
zh: 她向BBC世界频道《人们在解决世界问题》节目主持人玛拉·阿努比讲述了这一切。

text: Another benefit is MyPolis has designed the programme so it can be included as part of the national curriculum rather than as an add -on.
zh: 另一个好处是，MyPolis把这个项目设计成可以纳入国家课程体系的一部分，而不仅仅是附加内容。

text: Well, it's good to know that this is happening in other places, but the thing that also really strikes me here is that politicians are actually listening to these kids and taking them seriously,
zh: 很高兴知道其他地方也在这样做，但让我印象特别深的是，政治家们真的在倾听这些孩子的声音，并认真对待他们，

text: giving them answers because politicians, as we know, can be hard to pin down.
zh: 给他们答复，因为我们都知道，政治家其实是很难被“钉住”问出实话的。

text: The MyPolice programme is designed as part of the whole school curriculum and not just an add -on, meaning something extra that is added later instead of being planned in from the start.
zh: MyPolis这个项目被设计为整个学校课程体系的一部分，而不仅仅是附加内容，也就是说，不是事后临时添加的东西，而是从一开始就被规划进去的。

text: Myra was impressed that local politicians take the school children seriously.
zh: 玛拉对当地政治家认真对待学校孩子们这一点印象深刻。

text: If you take someone or or something seriously, you think it's important and deserves attention.
zh: 如果你认真对待某人或某事，意味着你认为它很重要，值得关注。

text: And that's good because, as everyone knows, politicians can be hard to pin down.
zh: 这很好，因为众所周知，政治家是很难被“问住”的。

text: To pin someone down means to force them to explain their plans and intentions in detail.
zh: “to pin someone down”意思是迫使某人详细说明他们的计划和意图。

text: It sounds like the programme is really developing active citizens of the future in Portugal.
zh: 听起来，这个项目确实在培养葡萄牙未来的积极公民。

text: In fact, it's been so successful that similar programmes have been started in Italy, Hungary and Mozambique.
zh: 事实上，它已经取得了很大成功，类似的项目也已经在意大利、匈牙利和莫桑比克启动。

text: OK, Neil, I think I should reveal the answer to my question.
zh: 好了，尼尔，我想该公布我那个问题的答案了。

text: I asked you what is the meaning of the ancient Greek word polis and you said people, which was the wrong answer, I'm afraid.
zh: 我问你古希腊词“polis”是什么意思，你回答说是“人民”，恐怕答错了。

text: The right answer was c city.
zh: 正确答案是C，城市。

text: Oh no.
zh: 哦，不。

text: Right, it's time to recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with the law of the land, a term for the set of rules and laws that exist in a country.
zh: 好的，现在是时候回顾一下我们今天学到的词汇了，首先是“the law of the land”，指一个国家现行的一整套规则和法律。

text: An erosion of trust happens when people gradually lose confidence in political and economic institutions.
zh: “信任的侵蚀”指人们逐渐失去对政治和经济机构的信心。

text: If a government is in office, it means they are currently in power.
zh: 如果一个政府“in office”，意味着它目前正在掌权。

text: An add -on is an extra part that is added to something later, rather than being built in from the start.
zh: “add-on”指后来附加上去的部分，而不是从一开始就被纳入设计的内容。

text: If you take someone seriously, you consider them important and worthy of your attention.
zh: 如果你“认真对待”某人，意味着你认为他们很重要，值得你关注。

text: And finally, if you find it hard to pin someone down, it's difficult to find out their specific plans and intentions.
zh: 最后，如果你觉得很难“pin someone down”，意味着很难弄清楚他们具体的计划和意图。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember you'll find a quiz and worksheet for this episode over on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到了，不过别忘了，你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期的测验和练习题。

text: See you there soon.
zh: 我们很快再见。

text: Bye.
zh: 再见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。
